qna.question: What do IIT Delhi hostels look like?

answers.author: RS
answers.qualification: Lives in banglore
answer: My sister was allotted 3rd floor as a fresher at Kailash hostel, at IIT Delhi. She was a bit disappointed at the time that there was no lift facility, and she had to carry her entire luggage all by herself.

She shared a two-seater room with two other girls because of lack of accommodation facilities. She and her roommates somehow made space for three beds and three table-chairs in that small room. The room was barely left with any space to move around. Thankfully, they got a balcony in which my sister managed to keep her cupboard. The room did not have any space for a cooler, and the hostel had a common washroom. A lack of cooler was very upsetting for her because of the harsh Delhi summers.

During her final year, got a single occupancy room on the ground floor, in the same hostel. But the room was still quite small, and she could barely fit in her clothes and shoes in the given space. The washrooms were still common.
Even though she fondly remembers her days at IIT Delhi, she is always vocal about her disappointment with the accommodation facilities of one of the premiere institutions of our country.

answers.author: SD
answers.qualification: M.Tech from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi (2017)
answer: IIT Delhi has 13 hostels in total, 11 boys and 2 girls. The hostels are well-equipped with all basic amenities. There are 24*7 water supply, electricity, and internet facilities. Most of the hostels have 3-4 floors and there are no lifts. Two of the hostels are newly constructed. These have lifts and better water facilities.
IIT Delhi hostel rooms are clean and well-maintained. But students need t clean their rooms by themselves. There are washrooms attached to each rooms. Washrooms are cleaned daily.
The mess timing is fixed. But the night canteen stays open until 3:30 am.

answers.author: TD
answers.qualification: B.Tech from Indian Institutes of Technology (2021)
answer: There are generally two types of rooms in IIT Delhi hostels.
Big rooms that are meant for two or three students.
Small rooms for one or two occupancies.

Due to the increasing number of students every year new hostels are made and in old hostels, two students live in rooms meant for a single person and three students share the room made for two-person. New hostels are made to meet the IIT standards but old hostels are quite congested and in need of maintenance.

The facilities in the hostel rooms include Fan, LAN port, Charging socket, Almirah, Bed, Table, and Chair. Bathrooms are common. There are water filters on the ground floor. If you get a room near the Wi-Fi router then you can enjoy high-speed internet. The night canteen stays open till 3:30 a.m. and breakfast is served at 7:15 a.m. The food provided in the mess is hygienic.
Hostels also have a big student hall, garden, badminton court, TT room, and other facilities.

qna.question: Who are the best professors at IIT Delhi, and why?

answers.author: PS
answers.qualification: B.Tech Chemical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi (2020)
answer: The answer to these types of questions is prone to subjectivity. All the professors in IIT Delhi are highly qualified and well-experienced in their respective fields. Which makes it difficult to mansion the best professor at IIT Delhi. Mentioned below are a few professors who are popular among the students.
Prof. Ambuj Sagar- He never imposes his own view on the students and lets you think for yourself and make your own conclusions so that you are not left with any doubts.

Professor Vijayraghavan M. Chariar- He teaches Technological Alternatives for Rural Development. His unique way of teaching makes you grow as a person and widen your perspective. He believes in immersive experimental learning and organizes day trips during class timings so that his students can learn from real-life experiences. He is also an entrepreneur, having managed to bring about his own start-up Ekam Eco Solutions, where he promotes sustainable solutions to many different environmental problems.

Professor Paresh Choksi- He is among one of the brilliant minds in the Chemical Engineering department. Students thoroughly enjoy his course on Fluid Mechanics for his methodical and organized way of teaching.
Professor Anupam Sukla- He has a unique approach towards teaching which is focused in maximizing the learning experience of the students.
Professor M R Ravi- His course on Human Values and technology is completely discussion-based. For students, his course becomes a journey of self-discovery.
There are also many other professors who are worth mentioning, but the list is quite long.

qna.question: How was your experience and life at IIT Delhi?

answers.author: KS
answers.qualification: B.Tech. from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: The overall time spent at IIT Delhi is an exhilarating experience you will never forget in your life. The sense of freedom, new friends, and a new city will completely transform you. You will gain knowledge of both academics and life-related issues.

You will make new friends in the hostel that will be with you in every struggle you face in your daily life be it academically or socially. The seniors will be your guiding light and teach you things that will save you from making foolish decisions and advice on important matters. In general everyone you will meet will be so full of knowledge and abilities that your motivation will always touch the sky. Some were fabulous sportspeople and taught winning strategies.
The teachers are the most cherished treasures of IIT Delhi. They are the best you can find and their humble aura and dress up will not let you realize their achievements and knowledge bar.

At IIT Delhi you can experience college life in the real sense. The cultural fests, night outs, parties and so many Clubs to join like BSW and BRCA make you create tons of memories. You will be left with memories of hanging out with friends at favorite spots of IITIans like Priya and PVR Saket, Hauz Khas village, SDA market, Pragati maidan, and Select Walk.
Overall, the time spent at IIT Delhi will groom your personality in the real sense making you smarter, wiser, and more humble than ever.

qna.question: How is the mess food at IIT Delhi?

answers.author: AS
answers.qualification: Masters from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: IIT Delhi has a total of 18 hostels for men, and 2 hostels for women. Each hostel has its mess hall that serves decent quality food. The general trend in most hostels is Breakfast>>Lunch>=Dinner. The mess menu is decided in winter/summer semester-wise separately and generally doesn't change throughout the semester.

A variety of dishes are served and the taste is also very good. Additionally, there are night messes till 4 am where you can munch on paranthe, momos, and Chinese. Some eateries that serve excellent snacks are Hollistic for juices and shakes, Rajdhani, Chaayos, CCD, Staff Canteen, and many more. Some combinations of meals served in the mess are
Chowmein, Manchurian, dal, roti
Macroni, Omelette chana and banana
Chole Bhature, Rice

A schedule is followed for mess food in every hostel. In which, sometimes the items served may differ. But the variety and quality of the food are excellent making it one of the motivating factors for students.

qna.question: What is the average Package in IIT delhi in BTECH?

answers.author: SG
answers.qualification: studied at Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: During the 2022 placement season at IIT Delhi, B.Tech graduates received an average package of around 16-17 LPA. However, the actual package offered can vary greatly depending on several factors such as the student's branch, academic performance, skills, and the hiring company. Students from popular branches such as computer science, electrical engineering, and mechanical engineering have been known to receive much higher packages in the range of 30-40 lakhs per annum or even more.

On the other hand, some students from less popular branches may receive lower packages in the range of 8-10 lakhs per annum. While the package offered is a significant factor, students must also consider other aspects such as the job role, work culture, and growth opportunities while selecting a company.

answers.author: SV
answers.qualification: B.Tech in Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi (Graduated 2021)
answer: IIT Delhi has a great placement record. A number of top companies like Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, Amazon, Uber, Google, Deloitte, IBM, Infosys, Qualcomm, Intel, etc recruit students from IIT Delhi. In 2019, the institute received 782 job offers, including186 PPOs.Microsoftprovided job offers to30 students, including two international offers. The average package at the institute is around 16 lakh per annum. Also, there has been a 15% increase in the number of companies visiting the campus for recruitment.

IIT Delhi provides placements in many sectors. Students are offered a variety of job profiles in different sectors such as Analytics, Consulting, Core, Finance, Management, Information Technology, and Research, etc. Core and IT Sectors are the sectors that offer the most offers, with 28% and 26% offers respectively.

qna.question: How are the hostel rooms in IIT Delhi?

answers.author: KS
answers.qualification: B.Tech. from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: IIT Delhi has a total of 18 hostels for men, and 2 hostels for women. Each hostel is equipped with basic amenities such as a common room, a reading room, and mess hall. The hostels also have 24-hour security and medical facilities. Additionally, the hostels have a common room, an indoor games room, and a gym.

The buildings are old given the time they were built, yet the facilities are decent. First-year students are allotted triple occupancy rooms while some are allotted double. This depends on the hostel you are assigned as each hostel has different maps and infrastructure. A typical room in an IIT Delhi has:
3 beds occupied by many
Three tables, mostly used for keeping bottles and random stuff
Three chairs, hardly used for sitting but keeping additional stuff
2 almirahs with four slots each.
3 wooden showcases for keeping books and stuff like toothbrushes , toothpaste, comb, empty perfume sprays,earphones etc.
2 LAN ports for 3 people with additional Wi-Fi facilities.
Electronic appliances include 2 fans, 2 tube lights, and 4 electricity boards.
All these basic amenities make students stay comfortable. After all, hostel life is all about making memories and new friends.

qna.question: Should I join IIT Delhi chemical or top NIT CSE?

answers.author: SG
answers.qualification: studied at Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: Yes, irrespective of the branch, choosing IIT Delhi will always prove to be beneficial for you. IIT Delhi has ties with many big firms that visit the campus for placements and offer great packages irrespective of your branch. One needs to be proficient in DSA and other computer science core subjects such as OOPS, OS, DBMS, and Computer Networks. A good grasp of these will fetch you a high-paying job no matter which branch you belong to in IIT Delhi.

Although most companies that visit IIT Delhi also visit NIT, your IIT tag will always fetch you extra benefits and no one can deny that. For instance, the same role offered to an IITiand will have more salary than that offered to a non-IITian.You can easily switch roles and fields given the brand name of the institute you are associated with. IITians even have more opportunities for higher studies in Foreign countries than non-IITians.
IITians especially from Mumbai or Delhi are always given preference over non-IITians hence it is advisable to go for IIT Delhi in any branch.

qna.question: Is the scope of IIT Delhi textile engineering good?

answers.author: DS
answers.qualification: B.Tech from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi (2022)
answer: My sister was a student at IIT Delhi in Textile Engineering and shared the disappointing experience that made her regret her decision a lot. The department is in its infancy and is the least demanded branch. Likewise, it offers the least average package at around INR 13 LPA and the lowest at INR 6 LPA.
Opportunities for a good job and package are scarce and the highest pay offered is even less than the pay of your friends with the lowest marks in schools
You will have to work hard and grasp a lot of concepts to maintain a good CPI only then you can target an MBA
The curriculum is rigid and does not challenge your learning and problem-solving capabilities.
The worth of the branch is so less that even your IIT tag does not come to your rescue during placements.
Most students go for non-core jobs roles like consultancy, data analysis or coding.
Few or no core job offers are made to the students of this branch
The branch does not do justice to the institute's brand name and thus I strongly recommend you against joining Textile Engineering at IIT Delhi.

qna.question: Have I done a mistake in choosing IIT Delhi mechanical over DTU CS? I preferred to do CS, but due to ego, I chose IIT Delhi.

answers.author: SG
answers.qualification: studied at Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: I don’t think anyone choosing IIT Delhi over any other engineering college has made a wrong decision given the opportunities and life experiences that come with it. While both are crucial, your choice of Institute is more important than your branch.
Here is a friend's journey for your reference l was a BTech student (Civil) at IIT BHU with a rank of 3600.
First, he completed a Nanodegree program from Udacity in Self Driving Cars and Machine learning
Next, he worked as a Derivative Trader in Future First where he performed Machine Learning and Deep Learning algorithms.
After gaining great experience and exposure he joined Quantela Inc for 2 Years where he worked as a Data Scientist.
Today, after a lot of job switches and immense experience he is working at Derq in Dubai as a Machine Learning Scientist and doing Online MS from Georgia Tech University in Computer Science.

This journey of a 7-pointer Civil Branch student to a high-tech CS company proves that branch does not matter in the end. He made the most of his campus life and kept struggling to switch his branch and eventually succeeded. That being said, you have made the right decision as only IIT Delhi can give you the right set of opportunities and exposure and not DTU.

qna.question: Why should someone choose IIT Delhi over IIT Mumbai for CSE? Except his/her AIR.?

answers.author: RR
answers.qualification: Works at Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi
answer: There are two sides to every coin. Similarly, there are many reasons why one must choose IIT Delhi over IIT Mumbai and vice versa. To make it further understandable, I have listed some common pros and cons of IIT Delhi
Pros
You are in proximity to the capital city
It’s easy to prepare for some other exams also apart from Engineering like IAS
Hauz Khas village in itself is an absolute treat to the eyes
Being in Delhi makes it possible to go for road trips to Jaipur, Chandigarh, Lansdown, Rishikesh, Dehradun, Agra, etc.
The Delhi Metro makes commuting quiet easy
There are many places to explore and hang out with your friends in the vicinity of the campus.
Cons
CS department of IIT Bombay is way better than IIT Delhi offering a plethora of diverse courses.
The campus of IIT Bombay has more natural landscape and greenery in comparison to IIT Delhi. This enhances the climate and overall ambiance inside the campus
Unlike IIT Delhi, the hostel facilities are much better at IIT Bombay while Delhi hostels are overly crowded.
Delhi faces extreme climatic conditions whereas the weather of IIT Bombay somehow remains the same throughout the year.
I leave it to you to weigh the pros and cons accordingly and make an informed decision.

about.value: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi is an autonomous institute of national importance, established in 1961. IITD has two satellite campuses located in Sonipat and Jhajjar. The international campus of IIT Delhi is located in Abu Dhabi. It has been ranked 2nd in India by Collegedunia in the engineering category in 2025. IIT Delhi NIRF Ranking is 2nd in India under the engineering category in 2024. NIRF ranked IIT Delhi 4th in the overall category in 2024. IIT Delhi offers admission to UG, PG, and PhD programs in multiple specializations. The flagship program at IIT Delhi is B.Tech. IIT Delhi accepts JEE Advanced scores, followed by JoSAA counseling for admission to the B.Tech program. M.Tech Admission at IIT Delhi is based on GATE scores, followed by COAP counseling. Candidates are required to appear for a Written Test or an Interview conducted by IIT Delhi for M.Tech admission. Apart from B.Tech & M.Tech programs, IIT Delhi also offers B.Des, MBA, M.Des, M.Sc, PhD & PG Diploma courses. IIT Delhi Placement 2026 is currently ongoing. As per the official media release by IIT Delhi, 1275 students secured job offers, including pre-placement offers (PPOs), with over 1,140 individual students placed. Over 300 PPOs were received this year. According to the IIT Delhi NIRF Report 2025, the median package stood at INR 19 LPA for UG programs and INR 18.82 LPA for PG programs. Top recruiters at IIT Delhi included American Express, Barclays, BCG, Goldman Sachs, Google, Microsoft, Ola, Oracle, and PayU.

content.value: What IIT Delhi certificate course can I apply for?
content.value_html: What IIT Delhi certificate course can I apply for?

value: IIT Delhi Certificate Course: IIT Delhi offers seven certificate programs in online mode. Digital marketing, data science, human resource management, business management, industrial design, design thinking and innovation, innovation and entrepreneurship, and project management are a few of these. Candidates have to appear for a screening test conducted by the institute.

IIT Delhi Course: In addition to certificates, the institute provides UG and PG courses in a range of subjects, including engineering, management, and design. Students can enroll in B.Tech and B.Des programs at the undergraduate level, and M.A., M.Tech, and M.Sc. programs are among the options available at the graduate level. Candidates must fill out the application form in order to apply for these certificates or other courses. This form and the necessary application fee must be submitted online or offline as part of the admissions process.

content.value: What is the duration of the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: What is the duration of the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi?

content.value: IIT Delhi offers a four-year B.Tech program. There are eight semesters in this full-time program. Students take departmental core and elective courses as well as foundational courses in the basic sciences, humanities, engineering sciences, and social sciences throughout the program. Undergraduate students can also take advantage of internship opportunities offered by IIT Delhi; short-term internships last roughly 1.5 to 2 months, while long-term internships last up to six months.
IIT Delhi also provides an integrated B.Tech and M.Tech program. In contrast to the typical four-year BTech and two-year MTech program durations, this dual degree program lasts five years.
Is there an IIT Delhi Executive MBA program?
content.value_html: Is there an IIT Delhi Executive MBA program?

value: IIT Delhi Executive MBA program: IIT Delhi provides an Executive MBA program through its Department of Management Studies (DMS).

IIT Delhi Executive MBA Eligibility Criteria: To be eligible, candidates need at least a 60% aggregate in their graduation (or 55% for reserved categories). Additionally, a minimum of 2 years of work experience after graduation is required. For a specialization in Technology Management, a background in fields such as Engineering, Technology, Physical Sciences, Statistics, Economics, Commerce, CA, or ICWAI is necessary. S
IIT Delhi Executive MBA Selection Criteria: Selection is determined through a valid CAT score and a Personal Interview.

content.value: Is IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay better for pursuing a B.Des degree?
content.value_html: Is IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay better for pursuing a B.Des degree?

content.value: A number of factors influence the decision of choosing between IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay for a B.Des degree. The UCEED entrance exam is required for admission to both institutions. The B.Des program at IIT Delhi lasts four years and is offered full-time. On the other hand, IIT Bombay provides a four- to five-year program through its Industrial Design Centre (IDC).

In terms of the UCEED 2025 cutoff rankings, IIT Delhi has a somewhat easier chance of admission due to its relatively higher cutoff rank, while IIT Bombay is more competitive with a lower cutoff rank for the General AI category.

In the General AI category, IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay had respective UCEED Round 2 cutoff scores of 35 and 14. The two institutes are contrasted below according to additional criteria, such as program fees and placements:

toc_sections.section: IIT Delhi Campus

content.value: Watch this video to get an understanding of the IIT Delhi Campus. In this video, you will get to see the various locations of IIT Delhi Campus, such as the Central Library, Lecture Hall Complex, Sports Complex, Hostels, etc.

IIT Delhi campus is well-equipped with the required amenities to provide a pleasant and engaging living environment for all students. The various facilities at IIT Delhi include a central library, hostels, a sports complex, a cafeteria, etc. Candidates can check the campus images below:

value: Central Library: It has a large collection of approximately 2 Lakh books, journals, and internet resources to help with learning and teaching. It also provides a great environment for learning and development. Students can schedule their preparation time in the central library and can utilize all the required resources.

Guest Houses: IIT Delhi offers well-furnished guest room facilities to accommodate visitors, such as bedding, air conditioning, Wi-Fi connectivity with attached bathrooms, and dining services. The facilities provided ensure a comfortable stay for guests, including visiting faculty, alumni, and parents of students.

Sports Complex: IIT Delhi has a wide range of sporting facilities, which includes sports tracks and playgrounds. IIT Delhi has a 400-meter racetrack and a walking/running track on the main playground. Faculty and their families have access to a variety of facilities, including athletic fields, cricket, hockey, football fields, basketball courts, clay and synthetic tennis courts, volleyball courts, an Olympic-sized swimming pool, a snooker table, a gym with separate rooms for men and women, and athletic fields.
Health services: To ensure the well-being of the campus community, the IIT Delhi Hospital provides medical and emergency services to students, staff, and faculty.

Computer Services Centre (CSC) and Classroom: The Indian Institute of Technology Delhi is equipped with high-performance computing facilities. The Center supports many computational studies and provides Internet services throughout the campus. IIT Delhi classrooms are equipped with projectors, podiums, speakers, etc., to conduct lectures and presentations.
Cafeteria and Dining: IIT Delhi has multiple cafeterias and student messes, which provide a variety of food options for students who come from multiple Indian cultures.
Student Activity Center (SAC): The SAC is a hub for extracurricular activities, housing clubs, and societies that cater to diverse interests such as robotics, drama, and music.
Innovative and Research Facilities: The Indian Institute of Technology Delhi has multiple laboratories and research centres that promote research and innovation.
Hostel: The Board for Hostel Management (BHM) manages 17 hostels (12 for males, 4 for girls, and 1 transit accommodation for girls), which house around 7000 of the 13000 students.

content.value: IIT Delhi hosts a range of events and festivals to enrich campus life. Rendezvous is an annual cultural festival, one of the largest in India, that includes music, dance, theatrical, and literary events. Tryst is a technical festival that features engineering and technology projects, workshops, and contests. The Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) hosts activities such as seminars, presentations, and competitions to promote a start-up culture. The Sports Meet is an annual event that celebrates athletic ability and team spirit.
Watch this video to know more about Anveshan 2025, an event hosted by IIT Delhi to exhibit the latest innovations and research.
What are the IIT Delhi hostel accommodations for students?
content.value_html: What are the IIT Delhi hostel accommodations for students?

content.value: IIT Delhi provides decent hostel accommodation to all its students. These hostels, originally built in the 1960s, are well-maintained. For first-year B.Tech students, the rooms are typically 3-seater, although some hostels still have 2-seater rooms due to varying infrastructure across the 17 hostels. The rooms might feel a bit cramped if you plan to spend all your time inside, but that’s not usually the case. Here's a review from a recent graduate about the hostel amenities:

"For a 2-person room, there are 2 large cupboards with long doors, 2 shoe racks with doors and 2 additional open cupboards, 2 long mirrors, 2 study tables, 2 beds, 2 ceiling fans, 2 tubelights. The meal quality is average, but it depends on the person who made the menu. Each hostel has different menus and chefs. Registration for the mess is done along with registration for the semester. In the E-scorner app, we pay the fee for it.” Read more
Which institute offers better hostel amenities for BTech students: IIT Bombay or IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Which institute offers better hostel amenities for BTech students: IIT Bombay or IIT Delhi?

content.value: IIT Delhi is generally thought to have better hostel facilities than IIT Bombay, based on comments from students and alumni. IIT Delhi's hostels are commended for their neat common areas, roomy accommodations, and cleanliness. Students enjoy the campus's first-rate sports facilities, gyms, and recreation areas. However, although the hostels at IIT Bombay are thought to be good, they are occasionally criticized for being overcrowded and lacking certain amenities. IIT Bombay does, however, provide several facilities, such as a student activity center, a sports complex, and a swimming pool.

Both IIT Bombay and IIT Delhi are renowned for their engineering programs and offer excellent hostel facilities. However, there are a few differences in amenities and costs that might influence your decision.
Note: These fees are based on previous years and may vary. Refer to official sources for the most current information.
Is it mandatory for students of IIT Delhi to stay in the institute's hostels?
content.value_html: Is it mandatory for students of IIT Delhi to stay in the institute's hostels?

content.value: Students don't need to stay in an IIT Delhi hostel. Hostel accommodations are not offered to students who live within a 15-kilometer radius. There are enough hostels on campus for those who want to stay: five for girls (including one for transit accommodation) and twelve for boys. The 325-acre, technologically sophisticated campus of IIT Delhi is one of the best in India, with great facilities and infrastructure. The Bharti School of Telecommunication Technology & Management, Amar Nath & Shashi Khosla School of Information Technology, Kusuma School of Biological Sciences, School of Artificial Intelligence, School of Public Policy, and School of Interdisciplinary Research are just a few of the institute's six unique schools.

content.value: Is IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay better for B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering?
content.value_html: Is IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay better for B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering?

content.value: When comparing Mechanical Engineering at IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay, both offer a curriculum similar to top-ranked international universities. At both institutes, this branch is in great demand. Both IITs provide first-rate infrastructure and have comparable QS rankings. While IIT Delhi is situated in the coveted Hauz Khas neighborhood, near the international airport and train stations, and is renowned for its green surroundings, IIT Bombay features a larger campus with a lake.

Placements shouldn't be a deciding factor because the placement trends at the two IITs are fairly similar. Although the difference is slight, IIT Bombay's Mechanical Engineering program normally opens and closes a little earlier than IIT Delhi in terms of admission ranks. Given Delhi's standing as a center for UPSC preparation and its handy location, students considering civil services may choose IIT Delhi over IIT Bombay based on personal preferences or historical trends.
Ultimately, both IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay offer similar opportunities for Mechanical Engineering, and the choice depends on individual preferences and local factors.
Which is a better choice between IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay for an MTech program?

When choosing between IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay for an MTech program, take into account the return on investment (ROI) based on average packages and course fees. This is a comparison of tuition fees and NIRF rankings:
NOTE: The fees mentioned are from an unofficial source and are indicative.

content.value: Should I choose Chemical Engineering at IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay, or CSE at IIT Gandhinagar?
content.value_html: Should I choose Chemical Engineering at IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay, or CSE at IIT Gandhinagar?

content.value: JEE Advanced cutoff for 2025 has not been released yet. However, the JEE Advanced cutoff for 2024 are available. For Chemical Engineering, the last round cutoff ranks were 2221 at IIT Delhi and 2545 at IIT Bombay for General AI category candidates. Meanwhile, for Computer Science Engineering (CSE) at IIT Gandhinagar, the cutoff was 1708.

Because of their strong faculty, research opportunities, higher NIRF rankings, and solid placement records with significant international corporations, IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay are generally great options for chemical engineering students. Though it might not have the same resources and standing as IIT Delhi and Bombay, IIT Gandhinagar also has excellent faculty and opportunities. IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, and IIT Gandhinagar are ranked 2nd, 4th, and 29th in the NIRF Ranking 2024, respectively. When choosing, take into account elements like ROI and placement.

content.value: Ques. Which course is best at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. Which course is best at IIT Delhi?

Further, the placement percentage of CSE students is very high when compared with other B.Tech branches.

content.value: Ques. Can I get direct admission to IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. Can I get direct admission to IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. No, a JEE Advanced is required to get admitted to the B.Tech program. The UCEED, JEE Advanced, GATE, IIT JAM, CEED, and CAT scores are used to determine admission to IIT Delhi and other programs such as BDes, Integrated MTech, MTech, MSc, MDes, and MBA. For the last round of selection, the institute uses a written exam and in-person interviews for admission to some courses.

content.value: Ques. Is it difficult to get into IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. It isn't easy to get admission into IIT Delhi. Getting into IIT Delhi is quite competitive as the institute's cutoff goes as high as the All India Rank of 3. There are opening ranks for each branch, and to be seated in that particular branch, one must meet the cutoff. IIT Delhi, one of the most well-known engineering schools in India, has been placed second in the NIRF best engineering college list for the previous five years running.

content.value: Ques. What are the costs associated with studying at IIT Delhi for Indian and international students?
content.value_html: Ques. What are the costs associated with studying at IIT Delhi for Indian and international students?

content.value: Ans. Since IIT Delhi is a public university, Indian students can typically afford its tuition costs. For Indian students, the entire cost comes to about INR 8–8.7 lakh. Hostel and mess fees, as well as other personal expenses, are not included in this amount. The annual tuition cost for international students is roughly $3,000. It can be costly to live in Delhi, particularly for people from outside the city. Students should have a budget of between INR 10,000 and INR 15,000 per month for living expenses, which include food, transportation, and entertainment.

content.value: The following IIT Delhi courses are options if your rank is up to 2500: Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering (Power and Automation), Chemical Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, and Integrated B.Tech. + M.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering, Integrated B.Tech. + M.Tech. in Mathematics and Computing, B.Tech. in Mathematics and Computing Engineering, and B.Tech. in Engineering and Computational Mechanics. These are based on the last round cutoff for the General AI category. Note that ranks can differ based on category and gender.
Ques. Does clearing the UCEED exam ensure a B.Des seat at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. Does clearing the UCEED exam ensure a B.Des seat at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Students must take IIT Bombay's UCEED exam in order to be admitted to IIT Delhi's B.Des program. The qualifying cutoff is determined by the UCEED exam's mean and standard deviation. Being on the admission rank list or just showing up for UCEED does not guarantee a spot in IIT Delhi's BDes program.
content.value_html: Ans. Students must take IIT Bombay's UCEED exam in order to be admitted to IIT Delhi's B.Des program. The qualifying cutoff is determined by the UCEED exam's mean and standard deviation. Being on the admission rank list or just showing up for UCEED does not guarantee a spot in IIT Delhi's BDes program.

content.value: After passing UCEED, candidates must apply separately to the UCEED participating institute for B.Des admission. Apart from passing the exam, applicants need to meet all eligibility criteria, provide necessary category certificates, and have their UCEED score verified by IIT Delhi.

about.value: IIT Delhi offers various programs at the UG, PG, and Doctoral levels. However, the flagship program of IIT Delhi is B.Tech. Admission to the B.Tech program is based on the scores of JEE Advanced, followed by JoSAA counselling. The UG courses at IIT Delhi include B.Des programs. Admission to the B.Des program is based on UCEED scores. Furthermore, IIT Delhi offers MSc, MDes, and MBA programs. GATE scores determine admission to the M.Tech program, followed by COAP counseling. Admission to the M.Sc. program is based on performance in the IIT JAM. IIT Delhi accepts the CEED exam score for admission to the M.Des program. A valid CAT score is required for admission to the MBA program. IIT Delhi also offers EMBA and MPP Programs at the PG level, and the selection is based on the interview conducted by the institute. Further, for the PhD admission, the institute accepts GATE/ CSIR/ UGC NET/ CEED/ ICAR/ DST INSPIRE Fellowship, followed by a personal interview.

value: IIT Delhi M.Tech Admission: Candidates must qualify for GATE and then register themselves for COAP counselling process by filling out the necessary details. IIT Delhi B.Tech admission: Candidates must qualify for JEE Main and then take the JEE Advanced exam. After qualifying, the candidate has to participate in the JoSAA counselling. During counselling, candidate has to select IIT Delhi as their preferred institute and choose their preferred B.Tech program. Seats are allotted on the basis of JEE Advanced rank, IIT Delhi cutoff, and student preferences. After seat is allotted, the students has to report to IIT Delhi for document verification and pay the admission fee to confirm admission.

IIT Delhi M.Sc. Admission: Candidates must qualify for IIT-JAM and register themselves for the JOAPS counselling. Students must specify their preferred institute and program while filling out the admission form. Selected candidates will have to qualify for the written test and interview conducted by IIT Delhi for seat allotment. After seat is allotted, candidates will be required to pay the admission fee with the specified time to confirm their admission.

IIT Delhi PhD Admission: Eligible candidates are required to submit online application through the official website of IIT Delhi. Shortlisted candidates will then have to qualify for the interview to pay the admission fee within the stipulated time for successful admission.

content.value: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Admission to IIT Delhi can be difficult due to limited seats and high competition. An aspiring student must aim to score at least 250-290 marks out of 360 to secure admission to IIT Delhi. The cutoffs are typically higher for well-known branches like CSE and ECE. Depending on the applicant's category and the engineering branch, different scores are needed. Aiming for at least 250 out of the 360 possible points in JEE Advanced can increase your chances of getting in. To guarantee a spot at IIT Delhi, concentrate on getting a high score. In 2025, the JEE Advanced cutoff rank was set between 125 and 12217 for the General category students. The closing rank for popular specializations such as CSE was 126, while the closing rank for Mathematics & Computing was set at 186. For other branches such as Mechanical Engineering the closing rank was 605, and the closing rank for Mechanical Engineering was 1861.

content.value: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?
content.value_html: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?

content.value: Ans. No, IIT Delhi does not currently allow direct admission based on Olympiad achievements. IIT Delhi B.Tech admission is solely based on the score obtained in JEE Advanced followed by JoSAA counselling. However, you can study B.Tech program at other IITs like IIT Madras, IIT Bombay, IIT Gandhinagar, and IIT Kanpur without having to qualify for JEE Advanced. Students are advised to check the official website of the respective IITs for more information regarding B.Tech admission at IIT without JEE Advanced through the Olympiad route.

After seat is allotted, the students has to report to IIT Delhi for document verification and pay the admission fee to confirm admission.

IIT Delhi M.Sc. Admission: Candidates must qualify for IIT-JAM and register themselves for the JOAPS counselling. Students must specify their preferred institute and program while filling out the admission form. Selected candidates will have to qualify for the written test and interview conducted by IIT Delhi for seat allotment. After seat is allotted, candidates will be required to pay the admission fee with the specified time to confirm their admission.

IIT Delhi PhD Admission: Eligible candidates are required to submit online application through the official website of IIT Delhi. Shortlisted candidates will then have to qualify for the interview to pay the admission fee within the stipulated time for successful admission.

toc_sections.section: IIT Delhi Admission FAQs

content.value: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Admission to IIT Delhi can be difficult due to limited seats and high competition. An aspiring student must aim to score at least 250-290 marks out of 360 to secure admission to IIT Delhi. The cutoffs are typically higher for well-known branches like CSE and ECE. Depending on the applicant's category and the engineering branch, different scores are needed. Aiming for at least 250 out of the 360 possible points in JEE Advanced can increase your chances of getting in. To guarantee a spot at IIT Delhi, concentrate on getting a high score. In 2025, the JEE Advanced cutoff rank was set between 125 and 12217 for the General category students. The closing rank for popular specializations such as CSE was 126, while the closing rank for Mathematics & Computing was set at 186. For other branches such as Mechanical Engineering the closing rank was 605, and the closing rank for Mechanical Engineering was 1861.

content.value: Ques. Is it Possible to get Admission To IIT Delhi without GATE?
content.value_html: Ques. Is it Possible to get Admission To IIT Delhi without GATE?

content.value: Ans. MTech admission to the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi is entirely competitive. Candidates seeking admission to the MTech program at IIT Delhi must take the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering. BTech graduates from IITs, on the other hand, are exempt from GATE and eligible for direct admission.

Candidates seeking admission to the MTech program at IIT Delhi must take the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering. BTech graduates from IITs, on the other hand, are exempt from GATE and eligible for direct admission.

content.value: Ques. What are the admission requirements for the MBA program at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What are the admission requirements for the MBA program at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Candidates must have a graduation degree with at least 60% marks in order to be admitted to DMS IIT Delhi's MBA program. CAT exam is the main factor used to determine admission. Candidates must take part in an interview round after qualifying CAT. Similar eligibility requirements apply to those interested in the Executive MBA program, with the CAT exam serving as a crucial requirement. The MBA program costs INR 11.2 lakh in tuition.

content.value: Ques. Is JEE Main score accepted by IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. No, JEE Advanced scores obtained by JEE Main-qualified candidates are accepted by the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi. The final selection for the IIT Delhi BTech course will be based on JoSAA counseling, which will determine seat allocation for all JEE Advanced-qualified candidates.

content.value: Ques. Is it possible to begin a Ph.D. program at IIT Delhi immediately after graduation?
content.value_html: Ques. Is it possible to begin a Ph.D. program at IIT Delhi immediately after graduation?

content.value: Ans. Other than B.Tech/BE/BS students with valid GATE scores, no other students can enroll in the Ph.D. program offered by IITD without a postgraduate degree in a relevant specialization.

content.value: Ques. What is the validity of GATE score for admission at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the validity of GATE score for admission at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. GATE Scores are valid for 3 years. Candidates will be given admission to IIT Delhi M.Tech and PhD programs based on the GATE score obtained in the last 3 years from the date of application.
content.value_html: Ans. GATE Scores are valid for 3 years. Candidates will be given admission to IIT Delhi M.Tech and PhD programs based on the GATE score obtained in the last 3 years from the date of application.

content.value: Ques. Does IIT Delhi require an entrance exam for admission to its PhD programs?
content.value_html: Ques. Does IIT Delhi require an entrance exam for admission to its PhD programs?

content.value: Ans. Yes, candidates must have a valid score in entrance exams like GATE, CEED, UGC, CSIR, DBT, or ICMR in order to be admitted into any of the PhD programs offered by IIT Delhi. The necessary exam may vary based on the specialization you select. Some departments may also hold an interview round after shortlisting candidates based on these scores.

content.value_html: Ques. What GATE score do I need for M.Tech admission at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Candidate’s GATE score must be at or above the cutoff marks in order to be admitted to IIT Delhi's M.Tech program. The M.Tech specialization and the candidate's category (e.g., General, OBC, SC/ST) determine these cut-off scores.

content.value: Ans. Candidates must fulfill certain eligibility requirements in order to apply to IIT Delhi's PhD program. A minimum CGPA of 6.00 on a 10-point scale or an aggregate of 60% marks is required for a Master's degree in disciplines such as engineering, technology, science, or the humanities. Interested parties should fill out the application form on the official website. Candidate’s performance in entrance exams like CSIR-NET or GATE determines their selection, which is then followed by an interview round.

content.value: Ques. Can a candidate take direct admission for an M.Tech program at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. Can a candidate take direct admission for an M.Tech program at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Yes, candidates can take direct admission to the M.Tech program if they have scored 75% marks or above in their Bachelor’s program in a relevant field with a GATE score. These candidates are exempted from interview rounds.

content.value: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?
content.value_html: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?

content.value: Ans. No, IIT Delhi does not currently allow direct admission based on Olympiad achievements. IIT Delhi B.Tech admission is solely based on the score obtained in JEE Advanced followed by JoSAA counselling. However, you can study B.Tech program at other IITs like IIT Madras, IIT Bombay, IIT Gandhinagar, and IIT Kanpur without having to qualify for JEE Advanced. Students are advised to check the official website of the respective IITs for more information regarding B.Tech admission at IIT without JEE Advanced through the Olympiad route.

IIT Delhi B.Tech admission: Candidates must qualify for JEE Main and then take the JEE Advanced exam. After qualifying, the candidate has to participate in the JoSAA counselling. During counselling, candidate has to select IIT Delhi as their preferred institute and choose their preferred B.Tech program. Seats are allotted on the basis of JEE Advanced rank, IIT Delhi cutoff, and student preferences. After seat is allotted, the students has to report to IIT Delhi for document verification and pay the admission fee to confirm admission.

IIT Delhi M.Sc. Admission: Candidates must qualify for IIT-JAM and register themselves for the JOAPS counselling. Students must specify their preferred institute and program while filling out the admission form. Selected candidates will have to qualify for the written test and interview conducted by IIT Delhi for seat allotment. After seat is allotted, candidates will be required to pay the admission fee with the specified time to confirm their admission.

IIT Delhi PhD Admission: Eligible candidates are required to submit online application through the official website of IIT Delhi. Shortlisted candidates will then have to qualify for the interview to pay the admission fee within the stipulated time for successful admission.

content.value: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Admission to IIT Delhi can be difficult due to limited seats and high competition. An aspiring student must aim to score at least 250-290 marks out of 360 to secure admission to IIT Delhi. The cutoffs are typically higher for well-known branches like CSE and ECE. Depending on the applicant's category and the engineering branch, different scores are needed. Aiming for at least 250 out of the 360 possible points in JEE Advanced can increase your chances of getting in. To guarantee a spot at IIT Delhi, concentrate on getting a high score. In 2025, the JEE Advanced cutoff rank was set between 125 and 12217 for the General category students. The closing rank for popular specializations such as CSE was 126, while the closing rank for Mathematics & Computing was set at 186. For other branches such as Mechanical Engineering the closing rank was 605, and the closing rank for Mechanical Engineering was 1861.

content.value: Ans. MTech admission to the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi is entirely competitive. Candidates seeking admission to the MTech program at IIT Delhi must take the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering. BTech graduates from IITs, on the other hand, are exempt from GATE and eligible for direct admission.

content.value: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?
content.value_html: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?

content.value: Ans. No, IIT Delhi does not currently allow direct admission based on Olympiad achievements. IIT Delhi B.Tech admission is solely based on the score obtained in JEE Advanced followed by JoSAA counselling. However, you can study B.Tech program at other IITs like IIT Madras, IIT Bombay, IIT Gandhinagar, and IIT Kanpur without having to qualify for JEE Advanced. Students are advised to check the official website of the respective IITs for more information regarding B.Tech admission at IIT without JEE Advanced through the Olympiad route.

MS Research Admission: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree in the relevant discipline with at least 60% marks (55% in case of reserved category candidates) along with a GATE Score. Final seat allotment is through COAP counseling followed by a Written Test and/or Interview conducted by IIT Delhi.
MPP Admission: Selection to MPP Programs is based on a written test and interview conducted by the institute.
MA/ PG Diploma Admission: Selection will be based on scores obtained in a bachelor’s degree.
EMBA Admission: Selection to the EMBA Program is based on a personal interview conducted by the institute.

toc_sections.section: IIT Delhi Admission FAQs

content.value: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the JEE score required for IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Admission to IIT Delhi can be difficult due to limited seats and high competition. An aspiring student must aim to score at least 250-290 marks out of 360 to secure admission to IIT Delhi. The cutoffs are typically higher for well-known branches like CSE and ECE. Depending on the applicant's category and the engineering branch, different scores are needed. Aiming for at least 250 out of the 360 possible points in JEE Advanced can increase your chances of getting in. To guarantee a spot at IIT Delhi, concentrate on getting a high score. In 2025, the JEE Advanced cutoff rank was set between 125 and 12217 for the General category students. The closing rank for popular specializations such as CSE was 126, while the closing rank for Mathematics & Computing was set at 186. For other branches such as Mechanical Engineering the closing rank was 605, and the closing rank for Mechanical Engineering was 1861.

content.value: Ques. How much CAT percentile is required for DMS IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. How much CAT percentile is required for DMS IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. MBA aspirants who want to pursue an MBA from IIT Delhi must score as high as 98 percentile to secure admission. In 2024, the IIT Delhi CAT cutoff was set at 98 percentile for the General category students.

content.value: Ques. Is it Possible to get Admission To IIT Delhi without GATE?
content.value_html: Ques. Is it Possible to get Admission To IIT Delhi without GATE?

content.value: Ans. MTech admission to the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi is entirely competitive. Candidates seeking admission to the MTech program at IIT Delhi must take the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering. BTech graduates from IITs, on the other hand, are exempt from GATE and eligible for direct admission.
content.value_html: Ans. MTech admission to the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi is entirely competitive. Candidates seeking admission to the MTech program at IIT Delhi must take the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering. BTech graduates from IITs, on the other hand, are exempt from GATE and eligible for direct admission.

content.value: Ques. What are the admission requirements for the MBA program at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What are the admission requirements for the MBA program at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Candidates must have a graduation degree with at least 60% marks in order to be admitted to DMS IIT Delhi's MBA program. CAT exam is the main factor used to determine admission. Candidates must take part in an interview round after qualifying CAT. Similar eligibility requirements apply to those interested in the Executive MBA program, with the CAT exam serving as a crucial requirement. The MBA program costs INR 11.2 lakh in tuition.

content.value: Ques. What exactly is the IDP M.Tech at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What exactly is the IDP M.Tech at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. IDP is an Interdisciplinary M.Tech program, a one-of-a-kind postgraduate program that combines various departments and interdisciplinarity.
content.value_html: Ans. IDP is an Interdisciplinary M.Tech program, a one-of-a-kind postgraduate program that combines various departments and interdisciplinarity.

content.value: Ques. Is JEE Main score accepted by IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. Is JEE Main score accepted by IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. No, JEE Advanced scores obtained by JEE Main-qualified candidates are accepted by the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi. The final selection for the IIT Delhi BTech course will be based on JoSAA counseling, which will determine seat allocation for all JEE Advanced-qualified candidates.

The final selection for the IIT Delhi BTech course will be based on JoSAA counseling, which will determine seat allocation for all JEE Advanced-qualified candidates.

content.value: Ques. Is it possible to begin a Ph.D. program at IIT Delhi immediately after graduation?
content.value_html: Ques. Is it possible to begin a Ph.D. program at IIT Delhi immediately after graduation?

content.value: Ans. Other than B.Tech/BE/BS students with valid GATE scores, no other students can enroll in the Ph.D. program offered by IITD without a postgraduate degree in a relevant specialization.
content.value_html: Ans. Other than B.Tech/BE/BS students with valid GATE scores, no other students can enroll in the Ph.D. program offered by IITD without a postgraduate degree in a relevant specialization.

content.value: Ques. What is the validity of GATE score for admission at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the validity of GATE score for admission at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. GATE Scores are valid for 3 years. Candidates will be given admission to IIT Delhi M.Tech and PhD programs based on the GATE score obtained in the last 3 years from the date of application.
content.value_html: Ans. GATE Scores are valid for 3 years. Candidates will be given admission to IIT Delhi M.Tech and PhD programs based on the GATE score obtained in the last 3 years from the date of application.

content.value: Ques. Does IIT Delhi require an entrance exam for admission to its PhD programs?
content.value_html: Ques. Does IIT Delhi require an entrance exam for admission to its PhD programs?

content.value: Ans. Yes, candidates must have a valid score in entrance exams like GATE, CEED, UGC, CSIR, DBT, or ICMR in order to be admitted into any of the PhD programs offered by IIT Delhi. The necessary exam may vary based on the specialization you select. Some departments may also hold an interview round after shortlisting candidates based on these scores.

The necessary exam may vary based on the specialization you select. Some departments may also hold an interview round after shortlisting candidates based on these scores.

content.value: Ques. What GATE score do I need for M.Tech admission at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What GATE score do I need for M.Tech admission at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Candidate’s GATE score must be at or above the cutoff marks in order to be admitted to IIT Delhi's M.Tech program. The M.Tech specialization and the candidate's category (e.g., General, OBC, SC/ST) determine these cut-off scores.

content.value: The minimum scores and rankings required for selection are outlined in the cutoff list. The general GATE cut-off score may typically fall between 350 and 800. Please be aware that this range should be regarded as indicative as it is based on unofficial sources.

content.value: Ques. What is the process for PhD admission at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. What is the process for PhD admission at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Candidates must fulfill certain eligibility requirements in order to apply to IIT Delhi's PhD program. A minimum CGPA of 6.00 on a 10-point scale or an aggregate of 60% marks is required for a Master's degree in disciplines such as engineering, technology, science, or the humanities. Interested parties should fill out the application form on the official website. Candidate’s performance in entrance exams like CSIR-NET or GATE determines their selection, which is then followed by an interview round.

Interested parties should fill out the application form on the official website. Candidate’s performance in entrance exams like CSIR-NET or GATE determines their selection, which is then followed by an interview round.

content.value: Ques. Can a candidate take direct admission for an M.Tech program at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: Ques. Can a candidate take direct admission for an M.Tech program at IIT Delhi?

content.value: Ans. Yes, candidates can take direct admission to the M.Tech program if they have scored 75% marks or above in their Bachelor’s program in a relevant field with a GATE score. These candidates are exempted from interview rounds.
content.value_html: Ans. Yes, candidates can take direct admission to the M.Tech program if they have scored 75% marks or above in their Bachelor’s program in a relevant field with a GATE score. These candidates are exempted from interview rounds.

content.value: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?
content.value_html: Ques. Can a candidate take admission for the B.Tech program at IIT Delhi based on Olympiad achievements?

content.value: Ans. No, IIT Delhi does not currently allow direct admission based on Olympiad achievements. IIT Delhi B.Tech admission is solely based on the score obtained in JEE Advanced followed by JoSAA counselling. However, you can study B.Tech program at other IITs like IIT Madras, IIT Bombay, IIT Gandhinagar, and IIT Kanpur without having to qualify for JEE Advanced. Students are advised to check the official website of the respective IITs for more information regarding B.Tech admission at IIT without JEE Advanced through the Olympiad route.

likes: My friend got a scholarship so i can verify it truly happens and is a great opportunity for students with low parental income to ease their parent's financial pressure
The college has really good placements, a lot of companies offer a lot of job opportunities and the packages are quite high as well.
Faculty expertise: iit delhi often attracts experienced and knowledgeable faculty members who are experts in their respective fields. students benefit from their guidance and mentorship.
Great placement record with good package
String alumini network across industries
Top notch infrastructures, lab and hostel

The campus is completely different from the whole city, it is more cleaner and is quiet peaceful with various different beautiful views at different points and is not too big so could easily walk through the campus.
Has really great architecture of the buildings and is surrounded by really intelligent people including some over the top profs and some really great minds from the country.
The night life is completely different and is one of the best part of staying in campus.
Peer group, faculty and people in general are very good and worth learning from
Maintenance of campus and division of labor is very good
Pro-student atmosphere is there with adequate help and support
The college infrastructure and facilities.
Laboratories for nearly all fields of physics
I liked their course curriculum and management.
They are student friendly , some professors are very friendly.
Many events are student driven only , that is important for management skills.
The classes and the labs are very well managed and are very advanced. The time table prepared by the proffs is really good and we get a lot time to study and do extra curricular activities
The social interections with juniors as well as seniors is very nice in our college. Due to it we get to know more people and improve our speaking skills
The faculties are very good in their respective subject with a great knowledge, also we have good placement in our college and we are also guided for it
First of all the fest in the college are really very awesome..There is a cultural fest that occur once a year (Rendezvous). This is a must watch fest for all the new upcoming students.

The sports culture at iit delhi is much more engaging and developed... People compete in the tournament names as GC(general championship)... Seniors help us to learn and play new sports which we never played earlier...

Hostel and it's life is really very awesome and loved to spend... The food in the mess is well managed by a committee of each hostel.. and seprate apartments have given to the mess staff so that there is no problem in working of the mess.
Campus infrastructure is quite good even if campus size is the smallest considering all iits but the infrastructure is quite unique and appreciable
The cultural activies and sports are quite well established. The seniors really know what they are doing and help the juniors to follow the path.
The girl to boy ratio is quite good due to girl reservation. It is about 1:3
The environment- nice plantations and comparatively less pollution than the city.
The classerooms- very big and futuristic classes with ACs and projectors
The events- many events like TRYST for techfest, RDV for cultural fest.
Good collage and good maintaining all good.
Faculty are perfect good teaching in the faculty
Good placement and good package in the collage
dislikes: The hostel facilities are not as good as expected the meal is too bad.
The race academy curriculum at iit delhi can be intense and demanding a leading to high levels of stress and pressure among students
75 percentage criteria is not good. moreover, classes are whole day from 8am to 6pm.
High academic pressure and competitive environment
Hostel and mess maintenance is required at times.
Flood normally occurs during rainy season
some of the hostels are quiet old and need renovation.
The courses are sequenced with bottleneck courses which if failed can disturb everything
Mess food quality is good in absolute sense but hygiene issues still noticeable
Certain faculty members are unruly and have to be dealt with care
The course load can be heavy some times.
Curriculum followed is too old and need to be revised
The one and most disliked thing is that they don't even give 1 day gap in exams .
Sometimes their attendance policy is very hectic.
The only thing i disliked is our hostels are very old as IIT Delhi is a very old college so new hostels must be made in our college

The one thing I dislike is the quality of teaching of the professors.. The lectures are sometimes really very boring to attend and students eventually lose interest in attending lectures and are much dependent on self study.

The second thing I dislike is about the size of the campus.. yes it is about 340 acres. But in comparison to other iits it is small. And hence there are not as big playground or activity centres or halls as compared to other iit s.

The third thing I dislike is about the fact that except of cycle no other vehicle is allowed for the students living in hostel...There are many big fines imposed if someone is caught ...Around 10000 if get caught with bike...
The quality of hostels are quite bad . Not recommendable
The relevance of subject taught in first year is obsolete
Transition from teachers to proffessors is very difficult . Teaching methods are very bad. Student teacher interaction is none in the class
The language barriers- most of the people speak Hindi, so the assumed language of conversation is Hindi between TAs and students or sometimes Teachers too.
Fast paced classes for slow learners compared to the toppers
Larger classrooms suggests less interaction between students themselves as well as with teachers.
No holidays sum times full boring in class room
Summer holidays is only 12 day so iam so sad
Sum subjects are understand but sum subjects are not understand

about.value: IIT Delhi is India’s top-ranking engineering college and is renowned across the globe for its quality education. The institute has been able to secure high rankings in overall segments, and also in fields like Engineering, Innovations, and Research. In the year 2023, NIRF has ranked IIT Delhi in the 3rd position in the overall area with a score of 82.16. Besides this, the institute stood at the 2nd and 3rd position in the Engineering and Research Rankings by the NIRF 2023. Furthermore, India Today rankings 2023 has declared IIT Delhi as the top engineering college in India as it has secured the 1st position.

IIT Delhi has also achieved global rankings by international ranking agencies like QS World University Rankings and QS Asian University Rankings. The institute stood at 197th position according to the QS World University Rankings 2024. Also, the institute has been ranked 48 in subjects namely Engineering and Technology by the QS World University Ranking by subject 2023.

content.question: Table of Content
content.answer:
IIT Delhi is India’s top-ranking engineering college and is renowned across the globe for its quality education. The institute has been able to secure high rankings in overall segments, and also in fields like Engineering, Innovations, and Research. In the year 2023, NIRF has ranked IIT Delhi in the 3rd position in the overall area with a score of 82.16. Besides this, the institute stood at the 2nd and 3rd position in the Engineering and Research Rankings by the NIRF 2023. Furthermore, India Today rankings 2023 has declared IIT Delhi as the top engineering college in India as it has secured the 1st position. 

IIT Delhi has also achieved global rankings by international ranking agencies like QS World University Rankings and QS Asian University Rankings. The institute stood at 197th position according to the QS World University Rankings 2024. Also, the institute has been ranked 48 in subjects namely Engineering and Technology by the QS World University Ranking by subject 2023.

IIT Delhi NIRF Ranking 2023

In the overall category, NIRF ranks 3rd at the All India level in the 2023 ranking. Further, In the engineering category, IIT Delhi has been ranked in the 2nd spot in the list of colleges. In the Engineering category, IIT Delhi has been toppled by IIT Madras for 1st position. 

In the research category, NIRF 2023 ranks IIT Delhi in 3rd position after IISc and IIT Madras. Moreover, IIT Delhi also stands out in the NIRF 2023 Management rankings and secured the 5th spot. The college finds itself among the top MBA colleges such as IIM Ahmedabad, IIM Bangalore, IIM Kozhikode, and IIM Calcutta. 

The parameter- wise wise score of IIT Delhi NIRF 2023 Engineering Ranking is represented in the graphical form below:

These institutes have been ranked in Engineering, Management, and Innovation departments. Given below are the comparison of Engineering and Management NIRF rankings 2023 of IIT Delhi and other IITs:

about.value: IIT Delhi Placement 2026 session is currently ongoing. As per the media release by IIT Delhi, 1275 students secured job offers, including pre-placement offers (PPOs), with over 1,140 individual students placed. Over 300 PPOs were received this year, reflecting a significant increase of over 33% compared to the previous placement cycle.

According to the IIT Delhi NIRF Report 2025, the median package for the UG 4-year program was INR 19 LPA, and for the UG 5-year program, the median package was INR 24.3 LPA. The median package for the M.Tech was INR 15.59 LPA. The top recruiters that participated in the IIT Delhi placement drive 2025 were American Express, Barclays, BCG, Goldman Sachs, Google, Microsoft, OLA, Oracle, PayU, etc.

As per the IIT Delhi MBA 2022-2024 Placement Brochure, the highest package was INR 40.5 LPA, and the average package was INR 23.4 LPA. The median package stood at 22.05 LPA. The top recruiters at IIT Delhi DMS MBA placement drives were Accenture, Amazon, Delhivery, Hexaware, KPIT, MAQ Software, Grant Thornton, Flipkart, Rockwell Automation, Wipro, and many others.

content.value: How successful are the BTech placements at IIT Delhi?
content.value_html: How successful are the BTech placements at IIT Delhi?

content.value: About 1,200 offers were made during IIT Delhi's Phase 1 placements in 2025, and 1,150 students were able to land jobs. Additionally, during the 2024–25 Phase 1 placements, over 50 international offers were made.
content.value_html: About 1,200 offers were made during IIT Delhi's Phase 1 placements in 2025, and 1,150 students were able to land jobs. Additionally, during the 2024–25 Phase 1 placements, over 50 international offers were made.

content.value: The median package for BTech placements in 2024 was INR 29 LPA, according to the Engineering NIRF report 2025. Previously, it was INR 17.6 LPA for 2021 and INR 20.5 LPA for 2022. According to research (unofficial sources), 96% of IIT Delhi BTech students were placed in 2024.

toc_sections.section: IIT Delhi Top Recruiters

about.value: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] Cutoff 2026 is expected to be released shortly by the official counselling authorities. Admission to highly sought-after courses like B.Tech (Computer Science and Engineering) and Bachelor of Technology [B.Tech] + M.Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) is extremely competitive and is based strictly on the JEE Advanced scores, followed by JoSAA seat allotment.

For students seeking admission to IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], analyzing the previous year's cutoff trends is the most effective way to gauge selection chances. The official closing ranks for the General, OBC, SC, ST, and EWS categories depend heavily on the total number of applicants, seat availability, and exam difficulty. Scroll down to check the detailed, course-wise opening and closing ranks for all major programs.
Table of Contents

about.value: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) for IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] Cutoff
Ques. What is the expected safe score/rank for B.Tech (Computer Science and Engineering) at IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]?

Ans. Based on the final official seat allotment data, students aiming for B.Tech (Computer Science and Engineering) through JEE Advanced should try to get a rank around 126 (for General, Male) to have a highly secure chance of admission.
Ques. Does IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] accept multiple entrance exams for admission?

Ans. Yes, students can get into IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] through different entrance exams like JEE Advanced, GATE, CAT, UCEED. Each exam has its own separate cutoff rank and official counselling process.
Ques. Which data point is more important: Round 1 opening rank or the Final Round closing rank?

Ans. The final round closing rank is the most important data point because it shows the exact rank of the absolute last student admitted. Round 1 opening ranks just show how competitive the course was at the very beginning of the process.
Ques. How do category reservations work for IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] cutoffs?

Ans. The college follows official government reservation rules. This means categories like OBC, SC, ST, and EWS have their own dedicated seats, and their closing ranks are usually lower and more accessible than the General (OPEN) category.
Ques. Can the closing ranks drop further during special rounds?

Ans. Yes, if seats are left empty after the main counselling rounds, authorities often hold Spot Rounds or CSAB special rounds. During these extra rounds, the closing ranks can drop significantly, offering a final chance for admission.

toc_sections.section: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) for IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] Cutoff

content.value: Ques. What is the expected safe score/rank for B.Tech (Computer Science and Engineering) at IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]?

Ans. Based on the final official seat allotment data, students aiming for B.Tech (Computer Science and Engineering) through JEE Advanced should try to get a rank around 126 (for General, Male) to have a highly secure chance of admission.
Ques. Does IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] accept multiple entrance exams for admission?

Ans. Yes, students can get into IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] through different entrance exams like JEE Advanced, GATE, CAT, UCEED. Each exam has its own separate cutoff rank and official counselling process.
Ques. Which data point is more important: Round 1 opening rank or the Final Round closing rank?

Ans. The final round closing rank is the most important data point because it shows the exact rank of the absolute last student admitted. Round 1 opening ranks just show how competitive the course was at the very beginning of the process.
Ques. How do category reservations work for IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD] cutoffs?

Ans. The college follows official government reservation rules. This means categories like OBC, SC, ST, and EWS have their own dedicated seats, and their closing ranks are usually lower and more accessible than the General (OPEN) category.
Ques. Can the closing ranks drop further during special rounds?

Ans. Yes, if seats are left empty after the main counselling rounds, authorities often hold Spot Rounds or CSAB special rounds. During these extra rounds, the closing ranks can drop significantly, offering a final chance for admission.

about.value: Students enrolled across various courses at IIT Delhi are offered several scholarship schemes and financial assistance to lessen their financial burden and to cover academic and other expenses during the entire program. The institute offers both external and internal scholarships to its students based on their qualifications and scores. However, a student needs to satisfy minimum eligibility criteria to avail the facility of scholarship awards and grants. Some of the popular scholarship schemes offered by IIT Delhi are as follows:
Merit Cum Means Scholarship:

value: The MCM (Merit Cum Means) Scholarships are offered to the students in the Undergraduate Programs of Engineering & Technology or M.Sc Programs.
Only 25% of the students are sanctioned to get this scholarship as per the institute's rules.
Students of IIT Delhi whose annual family income or parent's gross income is up to INR 4.5 lakhs.
An award of INR 1000 is awarded per month to the students belonging to the GEN/OBC/EWS categories and the recipients are exempted from paying the tuition fee.
The tuition fee is exempted to all the students of the SC/ ST/ PwD category.

about.value: Research Assistance

value: Research Assistance is provided to the students who are enrolled in the Ph.D. or research programs at IIT Delhi and are willing to work for the department.
Any student enrolled in the research-based programs on a full-time basis at IIT Delhi.
A minimum of INR 15,000 is awarded per month to students who can work about 20 hours a week.
For the students enrolled in MS Research Programme, a sum of INR 24,000 is awarded per month to work 8 hours a week.

about.value: Scholarships Offered by Alumni at IIT Delhi

Apart from the scholarships offered by the institute, many reputed alumni have instituted scholarships at IIT Delhi to support the students on the basis of their academic performances. Some of the scholarships offered by alumni, corporates, and philanthropists are mentioned below.

value: 2nd-year student in Electrical Engineering/ Power with 7.5 CGPA or above
1st-year student of M.Sc (Mathematics) securing a high CGPA at the end of the 1st semester
1st year UG Student based on JEE Rank & a minimum CGPA of 7.5
1st year UG Student of Electrical Engineering with highest JEE Rank

about.value: Loan Scholarships

Besides, most of the students are able to get the scholarship on the basis of merits and academic qualifications at IIT Delhi, still many alumni has contributed and instituted scholarships that repaid the interest on loans the student has took. Some of the loan scholarships are mentioned below.

value: Ved & Prem Lata Gulati Loan Scholarship
Shrimati Dharam Devi Bhatia Loan Scholarship
Samir Anand and Batch of 88 Loan Scholarship

about.value: IIT Delhi Scholarship FAQs
Ques. Is there any scholarship in IIT Delhi?

Ans. Yes, the students of IIT Delhi are offered various scholarships by donors and the institute itself such as Merit Cum Means scholarships and research assistance to help the students meet their finances.
Ques. Does IIT Delhi offer any loan scholarships?

Ans. Yes, the alumni department of IIT Delhi offers numerous loan scholarships to the students of IIT Delhi. Some of the loan scholarships are Ved & Prem Lata Gulati Scholarship, Smt. Sarada Warrior Loan Scholarship, Smt. Dharam Devi Bhatia Loan Scholarship etc.
Ques. Does an outside scholarship of IIT Delhi require a specific academic qualification?

Ans. Yes, scholarships offered by the alumni at IIT Delhi require a student to match the certain educational qualification to avail the scholarship facility such as a minimum CGPA of 7.0 is required for Amitraj Chibber Family Scholarship.
